title: Clouds
description: Optional cloud attenuation layers. Each layer generates an independent procedural cloud density field, binary mask, optical depth field, and transmission field. Multiple enabled layers are combined as an atmospheric stack: transmission multiplies, optical depth adds, masks are OR'd together, and density is combined as layered opacity.

type.description: Cloud layer type. Presets (`patchy`, `cellular`, `veil`, `sheet`, and `fog`) provide tuned defaults for cloud shape and optical depth. Use `custom` to start from generic defaults and directly specify model parameters.

name.description: Optional human-readable metadata label for this layer. The name is used in generated metadata and diagnostics only; it does not affect cloud generation. Defaults to `cloud_<index>`.

enabled.description: Whether to include this layer when generating the combined cloud field. Disabled layers are ignored as if they were not present.

seed.description: Optional deterministic seed for this layer's noise field. If omitted, SatSim derives a stable seed from the simulation seed, the layer index, and the cloud type so repeated runs are reproducible.

coverage.description: Target fractional cloud amount for this layer, from 0.0 for clear to 1.0 for full coverage. This is a fraction only; percent values such as 20 are not accepted.

feature_scales_m.description: Positive physical texture scales, in meters, used by the multi-scale procedural noise model. Smaller values add fine texture; larger values create broader cloud structures.

density_edge_width.description: Nonnegative softness width for mapping procedural texture into cloud density. Lower values create sharper cloud edges; higher values create broader, softer transitions.

density_floor.description: Optional minimum density added before optical-depth mapping. Use null for no explicit floor. Positive values are useful for veil, sheet, or fog-like layers where the frame should retain some baseline attenuation.

brightness.description: Optional cloud glow brightness in visual magnitude per arcsec^2, used to simulate artificial ground light illuminating the cloud. Lower magnitude values are brighter. Emitted photoelectrons are scaled by the inverted layer transmission, `1 - transmission`.

texture_contrast.description: Contrast applied to the normalized procedural texture before density mapping. A value of 1.0 preserves full texture variation; lower values flatten mottling and create smoother haze-like layers.

locality_degree.description: Positive integer controlling cloud support localization by multiplying independent texture fields. A value of 1 keeps broad coherent structure; higher values make cloud support more localized and patchy.

tau_min.description: Minimum optical depth used when converting nonzero cloud density into Beer-Lambert transmission. Higher values make even faint cloud regions more attenuating.

tau_max.description: Maximum optical depth reached by dense cloud regions. Higher values produce stronger attenuation and can make thick cloud regions nearly opaque. Runtime validation requires tau_max >= tau_min.

tau_gamma.description: Positive response exponent for mapping density into optical depth. Values below 1.0 make lower-density cloud regions more optically significant; values above 1.0 concentrate attenuation in denser regions.
